Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 057 [loan][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

55 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR ša₂ mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši A-šu₂ ša₂ mku-na-a

(o 1) 55 shekels of silver of Nabu-zer-ušabši, son of Kunaya, incumbent on Nabu-zer-ušabši, son of Bel-ušallim. From the middle of Ayyaru, 1 shekel of silver per mina will be added on his account.

o 22

ina muḫ-ḫi mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši A-šu₂ ša₂ mdEN—u₂-šal-lim

o 33

ul-tu lib₃-bi itiGU₄ ina muḫ-ḫi 1 ma-ne₂-e 1 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR

o 44

ina muḫ-ḫi-šu₂ i-rab-bi mim-mu-šu₂ ma-la ba-šu-u₂

(o 4) No other creditor can dispose of anything that is the pledge of Nabu-zer-ušabši until Nabu-zer-ušabši has received full repayment of his silver.

o 55

maš-ka-nu ša₂ mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši lu₂ra-šu-u₂ ša₂-nam-ma

o 66

a-na muḫ-ḫi ul i-šal-laṭ a-di mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši

o 77

⸢KU₃⸣.BABBAR-šu₂ i-šal-li-mu

o 88

lu₂mu-kin-nu mdNA₃—DA A-šu₂ ša₂ mdNA₃—za-kir mku-na-a

(o 8) Witnesses: Nabu-le˒u, son of Nabu-zakir; Kunaya, son of Nanaya-karabi; Šum-iddin, son of Balassu; Šakin-šumi, son of Aplaya and the scribe Šamaš-zer-iqiša, son of Upaqu.

o 99

A-šu₂ ša₂ mdna-na-a—SISKUR₂ mMU—MU A-šu₂ ša₂ mba-laṭ-su

o 1010

mGAR—MU A-šu₂ ša₂ mAa u₃ lu₂UMBISAG mdUTU—NUMUN—BAša₂

o 1111

A-šu₂ ša₂ mu₂-pa-qu UNUGki itiGAN U₄ 10-KAM₂

(o 11) Uruk, the 10th day of Kislimu, year 2 of Nabopolassar, king of Babylon.

o 1212

MU 2-KAM₂ mdNA₃—⸢IBILA₂⸣—URI₃ LUGAL TIN.TIRki

o 1313

⸢40?⸣ GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR ša₂ md⸢NA₃⸣—[NUMUN—GAL₂]ši A-šu₂ ša₂ mku-na-a

(o 13) 40 shekels of silver from Nabu-[zer]-ušabši, son of Kunaya, incumbent on Nabu-zer-iqiša, [son] of Balassu. From the 1st day of the intercalary month Addaru, 1 shekel of silver per mina will be added on his account, that is without the 1/3 shekel of silver received that [...] ... Nabu-gamil, son of Labaši-ili [...]....

o 1414

[ina] ⸢muḫ⸣-ḫi mdNA₃—NUMUN—⸢BA⸣[ša A]-šu₂ ša₂ m!ba-laṭ-su ul-tu U₄ 1-KAM₂

o 1515

ša₂ itiDIRI.ŠE.KIN.KU₅.DA ina muḫ-ḫi 1 ma-ne₂-e 1 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR

o 1616

[ina] ⸢muḫ-ḫi⸣-šu₂ i-rab-bi ša₂ la 1/3 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR maḫ-ru-u₂

o 1717

ša₂ [...] x mdNA₃—ga-mil A-šu₂ ša₂ mla-ba-ši—DINGIR

o 1818

[...]-x-u₂ mim-mu-šu₂ ma-la ba-šu-u₂ ša₂ IRI u EDIN

(o 18) No other creditor can dispose of anything, in the city or the open country, that is pledge of Nabu-zer-ušabši until Nabu-zer-ušabši has received full repayment of his silver.

o 1919

⸢maš⸣-ka-nu ša₂ mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši lu₂ra-šu-u₂ ša₂-nam-ma

o 2020

a-na muḫ-ḫi ul i-šal-laṭ a-di mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši

o 2121

KU₃.BABBAR-šu₂ i-šal-li-mu

o 2222

lu₂mu-kin-nu mib-na—diš-tar A-šu₂ ša₂ mdEN—SUba

(o 22) Witnesses: Ibnaya-Ištar, son of Bel-eriba; Ahheya, son of Eṭeru; Zerutu, son of Bišu; Nadin-ahi, son of Kudurru and the scribe Nadin-ahi, son of Kudurru.

o 2323

mŠEŠ-MEŠe-a A-šu₂ ša₂ me-ṭe₃-ru mNUMUNu₂-tu

o 2424

A-šu₂ ša₂ mbi-i-šu₂ mSUM.NA—ŠEŠ A-šu₂ ša₂ mNIG₂.DU

o 2525

u₃ lu₂UMBISAG mSUM.NA—ŠEŠ A-šu₂ ša₂ mNIG₂.DU! UNUGki itiDIRI.ŠE.KIN.KU₅.DA

(o 25) Uruk, the 4th day of the intercalary Addaru, year 2 of Nabopolassar, king of Babylon.

o 2626

U₄ 4-KAM₂ MU 2-KAM₂ mdNA₃—IBILA₂—URI₃ LUGAL TIN.TIRki

o 2727

5/6 MA.NA KU₃.BABBAR ša₂ mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši A-šu₂ ša₂ mku-na-a

(o 27) 5/6 mina of silver of Nabu-zer-ušabši, son of Kunaya, incumbent on Nabu-zer-ušabši, son of Bel-mušallim. From the middle of Ayyaru, 1 shekel of silver per mina will be added on his account.

o 2828

ina muḫ-ḫi mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši A-šu₂ ša₂ mdEN—GI

o 2929

ul-tu lib₃-bi itiGU₄ ina ⸢muḫ⸣-ḫi 1 ma-ne₃-e

o 3030

1 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR ina muḫ-ḫi-šu₂ i-rab-bi

Reverse
r 1r 1

mim-mu-šu₂ ma-la ba-šu-u₂ maš-ka-nu

(r 1) No other creditor can dispose of anything that is the pledge of Nabu-zer-ušabši until Nabu-zer-ušabši has received full repayment of his silver.

r 22

ša₂ mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši lu₂ra-šu-u₂ ša₂-nam-ma

r 33

a-na muḫ-ḫi ul i-šal-laṭ a-di mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši

r 44

KU₃.BABBAR-šu₂ i-šal-li-mu

r 55

lu₂mu-kin-nu mNIG₂.SUM.MU A-šu₂ ša₂ mdEN—ŠEŠ-MEŠ—SU

(r 5) Witnesses: Nidintu, son of Bel-ahhe-eriba; Ibnaya, son of Nanaya-karabi; Nabu-udammiq, son of Upaqu; Arad-Nabu, son of Nanaya-iddin; Šamaš-zer-iqiša, son of Upaqu.

r 66

mib-na-a A-šu₂ ša₂ mdna-na-a—SISKUR₂

r 77

mdNA₃—SIG₅iq A-šu₂ ša₂ mu₂-pa-qu

r 88

mARAD—dNA₃ A-šu₂ ša₂ mdna-na-a—MU

r 99

mdUTU—NUMUN—BAša₂ A-šu₂ ša₂ mu₂-pa-qu

r 1010

UNUGki itiNE U₄ 13-KAM₂ MU 1!-KAM₂

(r 10) Uruk, the 13th day of Abu, 1st year of Nabopolassar, king of Babylon.

r 1111

mdNA₃—IBILA₂—URI₃ LUGAL TIN.TIRki

r 1212

5/6 MA.NA KU₃.BABBAR ša₂ mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši A-šu₂ ša₂ mku-na-a

(r 12) 5/6 mina of silver of Nabu-zer-ušabši, son of Kunaya, incumbent on Nadnaya, son of Nabu-ušallim. From the 4th day of Arahsamnu, 10 shekels of silver per mina will be added yearly on his account and no other creditor can dispose of Ṣule-tašmu and his sons and anything that is the pledge of Nabu-zer-ušabši until Nabu-zer-ušabši has received full repayment of his silver.

r 1313

ina muḫ-ḫi mnad-na-a A-šu₂ ša₂ mdNA₃—GI ul-tu U₄ 4-KAM₂

r 1414

ša₂ itiAPIN ina muḫ-ḫi 1 ma-ne₂-e 10 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR ina muḫ-ḫi-šu₂

r 1515

ša₂ MU.AN.NA i-rab-bi u₃ fṣu!-le!-e-taš-mu u₃ DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂

r 1616

u₃ mim-mu-šu₂ ma-la ba-šu-u₂ maš-ka-nu

r 1717

ša₂ mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši lu₂ra-šu-u₂ ša₂-nam-ma

r 1818

a-na muḫ-ḫi ul i-šal-laṭ a-di mdNA₃—NUMUN—GAL₂ši

r 1919

KU₃.BABBAR-šu₂ i-šal-li-mu

r 2020

lu₂mu-kin-nu mdNERGALₓ(U.GUR)—SUM.NA A-šu₂ ša₂ mdEN—DINiṭ

(r 20) Witnesses: Nergal-iddin, son of Bel-uballiṭ; Bulluṭa, son of Bišu; Nergal-eṭir, son of Etel-Ištar; Ardiya, son of Iddinunu; Zerutu, son of Kunaya; Aplaya, son of Šaranni and the scribe Marduk-zer-ibni, son of Marduk-naṣir.

r 2121

mbul-luṭ-a A-šu₂ ša₂ mbi-i-šu₂

r 2222

mdNERGALₓ(U.GUR)—KARir A-šu₂ ša₂ me-tel—dINANA?

r 2323

mARADa A-šu₂ ša₂ mSUMnu-nu

r 2424

mNUMUNu₂-tu A-šu₂ ša₂ mku-na-a

r 2525

mAa A-šu₂ ša₂ mLUGALa-ni

r 2626

u₃ lu₂DUB.SAR mdAMAR.UTU—NUMUN—DU₃ A-šu₂ ša₂ mdAMAR.UTU—URI₃ir

r 2727

UNUGki itiAPIN U₄ 4-KAM₂

(r 27) Uruk, the 4th day of Arahšamnu, year 5 of Sin-šar-iškun, king of the land of Aššur, year of the locking of the gate.

r 2828

MU 5-KAM₂ md30—LUGAL—GARun

r 2929

LUGAL KUR aš-šur MU e-del KA₂
